---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-LAB01
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-LAB01
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-LAB01
id: ESE111-CH04-LAB01-en
resource_type: laboratory
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-LAB01-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
estimated_student_time_min: 150
safety_level: L1
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract:
  outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: pending-restricted-store
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# Laboratory LAB04 — Does the physical network match its schematic?

## Outcomes and operating envelope

In 150 minutes, pairs rotate builder, instrument lead, analyst, and verifier roles.
Each learner must personally direct or perform one inspection, choose a voltage
test sequence and DMM setting, record raw readings, update a fault hypothesis, and
defend the redesign. Manipulation assistance is allowed when it preserves those
decisions. Prerequisites are the readiness check and G1, or direct instructor
control before G1. This activity assesses a low-energy DC resistor network; it
does not assess DMM current mode, soldering, a real lamp, or a sensor product.

This source is a draft. Do not energize it until the local activity risk assessment,
apparatus path, supervision, and instructor authorization are approved.

## Safety and stop-work conditions

- Approved protected DC source only; 0–6.1 V, configured current limit ≤20 mA.
- Leads remain in COM and V/Ω. The current input is not permitted.
- Source output off and circuit verified de-energized for wiring or resistance work.
- Before first power and after any topology change, complete the inspection below.
- Stop for a damaged lead/part, uncertain node, unstable or unexpected reading,
  source limit indication, heat, odor, smoke, or any value beyond the approved range.
- Say **STOP**; switch output off if safe; do not touch until isolated; notify the
  instructor. Only the authorized instructor may release the setup after a stop.

Maximum credible circuit current after a hard short beyond R1 is about
$6.1\text{ V}/9.9\text{ kΩ}<0.62\text{ mA}$, but that calculation neither
authorizes the source nor replaces its protective limit and inspection.

## Apparatus paths and architecture

| Required evidence | Minimal path | Standard path | Equivalence boundary |
|---|---|---|---|
| source condition | approved current-limited source plus DMM | bench supply display cross-checked by DMM | learner records actual VS and configured limit |
| three node readings | one function-checked DMM, sequential | two DMMs or DMM plus approved scope for a static cross-check | learner chooses COM, function, range, order; screenshots alone do not pass |
| loading estimate | exact manual input resistance | exact manuals for both channels | same branch model and uncertainty decision |
| personal physical evidence | construct/inspect, connect, read, diagnose, redesign | identical | automation or a second channel cannot make the decisions |

Components: breadboard, R1=10 kΩ, R2=22 kΩ, R3=33 kΩ, baseline R4=15 kΩ,
redesign candidates 18/22/27/33 kΩ, all proposed 1%; labeled leads. Substitution
requires the same topology, measured resistance, rating margin, predicted range,
and a new review. The advanced path is not declared for this pilot.

![LAB04 controlled schematic.](../../shared/figures/ese111-ch04-network.svg){fig-alt="A 6 V source feeds R1 to TP-A; R2 connects TP-A to COM; R3 connects TP-A to TP-B; R4 connects TP-B to COM."}

## Pre-lab prediction

Before seeing any result, submit:

1. arrows/polarities and an order-of-magnitude bound for all currents;
2. nominal $V_A,V_B$ and both node equations;
3. expected qualitative signatures for an open branch and a wrong-value branch;
4. safe DMM socket/function/range, reference, and test-point order;
5. predicted direction of TP-B change when R4 increases.

The simulation screening bounds are 3.559–3.655 V at TP-A and 1.087–1.155 V at
TP-B for the baseline across declared source/resistor corners and 1/10 MΩ meter
models. These are not physical pilot ranges; staff must replace the pending range
record with measured expected intervals before ordinary teaching.

## Setup inspection checkpoint

With output off and zero energy verified, learner and verifier initial each line:

- [ ] activity revision, source ID, DMM ID, meter input resistance, and function-check record captured;
- [ ] leads undamaged and inserted in COM and V/Ω; DC-voltage function selected;
- [ ] supply output off, set to 6.0 V, current limit no more than 20 mA;
- [ ] breadboard continuity mapped; VS, TP-A, TP-B, and COM labeled;
- [ ] R1–R4 values measured out of circuit or otherwise isolated and recorded;
- [ ] topology traced against the controlled schematic; no unintended VS–COM short;
- [ ] resistor ratings and hard-short bound checked; work area clear;
- [ ] instructor checkpoint ID recorded before energization.

## Procedure and raw record

1. Create a run record conforming to `shared/data/raw-data.schema.json`; select
   `physical-measurement`, never `simulation` or `prepared`.
2. Record the individual pre-lab prediction without editing it after the reveal.
3. Build the baseline with output off and obtain the inspection checkpoint.
4. Energize without touching the network; observe supply state for five seconds.
5. Measure VS relative to COM; record sign, raw display, range, resolution, accuracy
   basis, uncertainty, and sequence before moving the probe.
6. Repeat the same complete record for TP-A and TP-B.
7. Switch output off and verify de-energized state.
8. Infer $I_1\ldots I_4$ from measured node differences and measured resistance.
9. Calculate signed KCL residuals at A and B, two loop residuals, and power balance.
10. Add the meter input branch; recompute B and decide whether disagreement is
    consistent with the stated uncertainty or needs investigation.
11. Ask the instructor to apply one controlled reversible fault while the setup is
    isolated; the public schedule reveals only `open-branch` or `incorrect-value-branch`.
12. Re-energize only after a new checkpoint, then record symptoms without replacing parts.
13. Rank at least three hypotheses and choose one test whose possible outcomes
    distinguish at least two; obtain approval, isolate, run it, and update the table.
14. After evidence supports a root cause, authorize/carry out the correction and
    rerun the baseline acceptance measurements.
15. Compare R4 candidates; select one with margin for TP-B=1.40–1.52 V and source
    current ≤0.25 mA, not merely a nominal pass.
16. Isolate, install the selected R4, repeat inspection and measurements, and accept
    or reject both requirements from physical evidence.

| Seq. | State/configuration | From→to | Raw value/unit | Range/resolution | Expanded uncertainty $U$ / coverage $k$ / basis | Prediction | Residual | Decision/anomaly |
|---:|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | baseline | VS→COM | | | | | | |
| 2 | baseline | TP-A→COM | | | | | | |
| 3 | baseline | TP-B→COM | | | | | | |
| 4 | fault present | selected test | | | | | | retained |
| 5 | corrected | TP-B→COM | | | | | | |
| 6 | redesign | TP-B→COM | | | | | | accept/reject |

## Diagnostic tree

Start with VS under load. If wrong, isolate source/configuration before the network.
If VS is sound, compare A and B with predicted signatures. Near-zero B with a
plausible A prioritizes an open link or shunt at B; high B prioritizes a weak/open
return branch or wrong value. De-energized resistance/continuity at the selected
branch is safer and more discriminating than random replacement. If all nodes are
close but residuals shift with probe connection, model the meter. At every branch,
state what result would reverse your ranking.

Exact insertion, randomization, and reset details belong to
`RST-ESE111-CH04-FAULT-001`, outside public history.

## Design acceptance and limitations

Pass the redesign only if recorded physical evidence and its expanded uncertainty
show $V_B-U_V\ge1.40\text{ V}$, $V_B+U_V\le1.52\text{ V}$, and
$I_S+U_I\le0.25\text{ mA}$ under the stated source, component, meter, ambient,
coverage-factor, and uncertainty condition. If an interval crosses a limit, report
the result as inconclusive and improve the evidence; do not round it into a pass.
A simulated 22 kΩ pass is the leading
candidate, not the result. Record any residual risk: contact instability, meter
input uncertainty, temperature, substitute values, or margin too small.

## Closeout and individual check

Switch output off, isolate, verify zero, remove leads, return reusable resistors by
value, quarantine damaged or suspect parts, and follow the local e-waste record.
Archive raw and derived data separately with configuration and checksum. Each learner
answers: “Which one measurement changed your diagnosis or design, and why would a
different result have changed it again?” The common 28-point rubric and targeted
reassessment route apply; safety and honest provenance are conjunctive.
